ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-10-293442 Conservatorship of Marguerite Jauch and PTR-10-293803 The Jauch Family Trust.
Appearances: Nancy Rasch for (conservator) Institue on Aging, representative Carrie Moulton present. Court Appointed counsel Judy Hitchcock for and with (conservatee) Marguerite Jauch. Lawrence Siracusa for and with (trustee) Keith Carlos.

The Court asked if anyone has heard from Mr. DeJong who is not present. Ms. Rasch stated only the letter that was submitted to the court. Mr. Siracusa confirmed that he did not receive notice of a court call request. Ms. Hitchcock asked Ms. Jauch and was told he may be out of state. The Court stated that the request for a 30 day continuance has been denied, that Mr. DeJong is not present in court and that he did not arrange to appear via court call. Ms. Hitchcock reported that Ms. Jauch has agreed to testing to assess her abilities and to determine the need for an ongoing conservatorship, that Ms. Jauch has ongoing concerns regarding the trust and the trustee, that she did not agree to Mr. Carlos as trustee and opposed his appointment, that Mr. Carlos has made considerable efforts to keep in touch and is very considerate of her needs and that there is not enough there to petition for removal. Mr. Siracusa reported that Mr. Carlos has gone above and beyond to keep Ms. Jauch informed, that he has followed her wishes in regards to the Hugo Street property, that he has fixed up her Marin property and that Ms. Jauch is able to go back and forth between properties, that Mr. Carlos has done a stellar job of managing the trust, that the concerns of Ms. Jauch ebb and flow possibly due to the interaction of others, that Ms. Jauch will not be happy with anyone as trustee and that she has a lot more input with Mr. Carlos than another trustee would give. Ms. Rasch stated that testing is a good idea, that no one has filed objections regarding the accountings and that once they get the test results they will be provided to Ms. Jauch's counsel and they can decide how to proceed. Ms. Jauch was given a chance to address the court and stated that she feels neglected and would rather not have the help. The Court took the accounting petitions in both cases under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Continued To Oct-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-10-293422 Conservatorship of Marguerite Jauch and PTR-10-293803 Jauch Family Revocable Trust.
Appearances: Arie DeJong (Objector) in Propria Persona.

These two matters had been pre-granted. Mr. DeJong stated that he has been a family friend for 60 years, that he visited Ms. Jauch last night and talks to her on the telephone daily, that she wants him involved in the estate of the revocable trust, that he has been in communication with her counsel who has told him to request guidance from the court, that Ms. Jauch is 92 years old and not in a position to appear in court and that he would like direction. The Court requested that Mr. DeJong file his objections and provide notice to all counsel with copies of whatever he files. The Accounting petitions in each matter were continued to October 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. DeJong was instructed to file his objections no later than one week prior to the hearing and that he may participate by Court Call if he chooses.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Keith Carlos. Judy Hitchcock for and with Margarite Jauch.

Ms. Jauch expressed her concerns. Mr. Shcultz addressed the commissions amount. The sale was confirmed at $215,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $204,000.00 is required.
